channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 84 replies — 336.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-06-06 21:01 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62320391
I LOVE those heckin cope threads. Like if some retard making a strong statement on this board is actually going to influence price action. Your brains have been fried by a decade of shitcoining and pump n dumping on other anons. Deal with the real world you absolute goy trash meats. We're going to 2 digits for btc wether u believe it or not.

62320405
>>62309503
BTC is not Bitcoin and basically worthless. Sheep will figure it out eventually, until then they follow the pedo coin BTC congrats

62320466
>>62320405
>BTC is not Bitcoin and basically worthless!!111111111
>my XYZ coin (0.0003 BTC) is real bitcoin!!!!!111111

62320468
>>62309503
The city of OP

62320500
>>62320466
>inb4 BCH is not Bitcoin either, only BSV is

62320588
>>62320500
only Bit-Diamond XD
at least they changed hash afair to not compete for same ASICs (with predictable result)

62320857
>>62320358
Good. At least now the dipshits will sell the bottom.

If you think you are getting the upperhand, you are not.

It is better for BTC to get rid of the financially illiterate than it to be worth 200k but held by imbeciles trying to scam each other.

Bitcoin is a storage of value. Want proof? Zoom out the chart.

We are winning the fight against bullshit Fiat currency, dwarven suckers, and boomer indexes.

Everyone talking shit here WISHES they had bought in years ago. Sour grapes everywhere

The BUYROG remains.

>>62320391
>cope thread

I made free money to anyone who wasn't dumb enough to now sell the bottom and make this a sucker's rally.

>>62320405
Was waiting for the inevitable BTC will go to zero comment.

It is the bottom signal.

62320897
>>62309535
>I don't need proof
>BTC 5k below your prediction.

Faggot.

62320920
>>62309503
OHNONONONONONONONONONONONONO LMAOOO

62323245
Any last words, OP?

62323584
>>62323245
>Any last words, OP?

62323600
>>62323584
>OP rn

62323880
>>62323245
>>62323584
>>62323600
YOU LOST, TRANNY

It doesn't matter if Bitcoin falls to 59k, I repeat what I said: it is good riddance that the yuppies go find another con to shove down people's throats because they are overleveraged and undereducated. No one wants their store value reserve being speculated on by retards who laugh as they sell the bottom, then complain they can't make money.

Stupidity is a tax on success.

Also, keep in mind, BTC might be 6% loss in a day, SILVER and GOLD are doing WORSE. You can't compare BTC performance against stock because it is not apples to apples, BTC can only be compared with other crypto in its asset class, and other storage value investment like PMs. And guess what? It still blows all of them out of the water, and the trade volume of BTC evaluated in any desk is far greater than all of crypto and rock collections combined. BTC brockerage platforms exist by the thousands, how many dealers of PMs exist?

I will put this even simpler for bear trolls: read the Bible, flip it open to the parable of the talents, and read what is said about the one faggot who thought he was smug and clever because he neither won, nor lost the money loaned to him. This is you, this is the whole bear market.

I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes. I'm going laughing my way to the bank enjoying this fire sale of BTC owned by the eternal underclass of statist peasants.

BTC will be 70k by the end of the month. Hell, it will be 60k+ by the end of the day.

If I called the bottom 10% above, it is because I overestimated the intelligence of the morons that do me a favor by forgetting this asset class exists.

May they buy ETFs and go hold the bag of billionaire scammers.

I repeat:
>if you bought, hold
>if you haven't, enjoy the sale
>if you sold, we are better without you

62323927
>>62323880
>I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes

62323956
>>62323880

62326531
The falling knife is around $32.8k

Screencap this and thank me later.

62326539
>>62309503
Heavy bag huh?

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

62327899
>>62326531
this guy fucks

62328894
You are missing the most obvious bottom in history. And you are proud of it.

Grim.

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

item.title: Am I fucked if I'm put on a PIP? — 55 replies — 111.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Am I fucked if I'm put on a PIP?

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-06-06 20:43 UTC

OP

Been at a fortune 500 company for 4 years. No problems until today. I'm a dev btw. Get random email from my manager today about a performance meeting. No mention of my performance prior to this aside from a comment about me not always being in the office as much and working from home.

Thing is I've been crushing it at work and been getting nothing but praise from clients and managers. Just last week the manager from the PIP meeting was telling me I'm being moved to another team because I completed so much work on the current one and they need my assistance with another dev team. Now he's saying bs out his ass about performance. And unironically while I'm on the call with him and hr today about this PIP bullshit another manager pinged me to tell me several clients were telling him how much they love working with me

It feels like the goals in the PIP are set up to make me fail. They basically want me working across 3 teams all at once now along with some online classes because I "lack skills" even though I've taken their skill assessment tests and scored proficient on them. I honestly hate the place so I'm not worried and I've got some investments I can liquidate to help me out with bills until I figure something out or get another job. I'm just so confused like what in the fuck caused this? Not only have I done what's been asked of me but I complete more work than others and that's easy to prove. They've got the metrics so what the fuck. Now I gotta look for another tech job in this bs industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62325457
Why were you on a PIP to begin with?
Sounds like you were underperforming before this current stretch

62325483
>>62325457
I just got placed on it today. No ones made a negative comment on my performance aside from me not always being in the office but I would still complete all the work including additional tasks without being asked. Feels targeted

62325486
They're trying to get rid of you for some reason. If I had to guess they're going to replace you with a team of Indians for a quarter of the cost

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326110
>>62325409
glad that I've worked for myself since 1993 and have no idea what a PIP is.

62326118
>>62326110
I only know because I lurk here

I started working for myself in 1999 and can't imagine putting up with half the shit people talk about here. I think back in our day we called it "getting written up," which was just your ten minute warning before getting fired.

62326230
>>62325409
yes, they’re absolutely going to fire you, they’re just getting their legal ducks in a row first. Start preparing all your evidence now that the firing is unjustified (emails from customers & management praising you, KPIs you’re meeting / exceeding) and give it to a lawyer once you’re fired.
Don’t sign the severance package that says you can’t sue, all the severance package does is saves them from paying you more in unemployment.

62326238
>>62325535
The answer isn’t important. A lot of managers used to be retarded (bell curve) but now nearly all are retarded.
It could be some female except 5 levels above you who has decided to axe the entire department or wants all white males fired. You just don’t know.

62326251
In the US an employee can generally be fired for any reason or no reason at all. As long as OP isn't fired for an illegal reason (discrimination, retaliation) he probably can't sue. Doesn't matter if he's great at his job. Employers are allowed to fire great employees if they want

62326255
it’s easier solely about RTO or they are planning to outsource your job. in my experience we’ve never put someone on a PIP unless we were planning to get rid of them. the PIP was just a formality to check all of the boxes.

62326289
>>62326251
discrimination should be easy to prove when they’re firing only white male developers and hiring only indians. Just ask to see HR’s emails concerning the layoffs where they got a directive from management to fire all the americans.
Also OP, take all your sick leave now and abuse unlimited PTO if you have it

62326300
That acre was listed at 220k btw unironically.

62326313
>>62326289
when OP said "replace me with another indian" I took that to mean he is also indian

62326314
>>62326300
If you zoom out is it actually in a decent area? That land value doesn't even surprise me.

62326349
>>62325409
It really varies by the individuals who are assessing you. That shit has happened to me three times:

1. shitty accounting software company whose CEO fucked up massively and they "fired for cause" literally 1/3 (33%) of the staff (CEO got fired by the board about nine months later, kek). They intentionally gave me wrong information on how to do certain tasks just so that I would fail the "PIP" -- when I had to ask one of my coworkers about some of the stuff, she looked at the version number for the Oracle package I was supposed to install and instantly threw a shitfit because she recognized the version number and immediately recognized that it was a version that didn't function with our codebase. I had started looking for a new job the moment the CEO announced "THE RUMORS ARE FALSE! THERE WILL BE NO LAYOFFS!" at his weekly all-hands inspirational meeting (he read a business book every week and then bloviated about them to everyone in the entire fucking company, wasting an hour of everyone's time because that's such a good use of resources). Anyway, the meeting in which they were going to fire me turned out hilarious because I literally got a job offer faxed to me 20 minutes before the meeting. My manager started telling me how horrible I was, I showed him the job offer fax, and he immediately backtracked and asked if they had any open slots he could apply to.

2. shitty dot.com company, dot.com implosion happened, my manager decided to fuck me over instead of letting me get laid off and get severance with everyone else. I did the PIP, he realized he still needed me for a couple more weeks until the product we were writing shipped, so I "passed" the PIP. Then he fired me anyway a few weeks later, telling HR that I hadn't done a task he'd assigned me even though he'd been telling me not to do it because "it's not necessary".

(continued)

62326394
>>62325409
>>62326349
3. shitty foreign company, manager found out I wasn't a Christian like he assumed (I don't believe in any of that shit but I don't talk about religion at all) so he began sabotaging me by simply not assigning any work to me, then said I hadn't completed enough assignments to stay. Then he pretended everything was fine until I took a trip home, and he fired me by email while I was out of the country, then he fucked up my visa to try to get me fined for overstay and deported. This was especially shitty of him because (i) I could have just taken all my belongings home and not come back if he'd fired me like a not-a-total-fucking-asshole, and (ii) if he'd gotten me deported for overstaying after I came back to pick up my stuff, everything I owned would have been thrown in the garbage by my landlord. Luckily, the Customs people didn't just rubberstamp things but actually figured out what had happened and decided not to fuck me over.

>It feels like the goals in the PIP are set up to make me fail.
Yeah, that's generally what they do when they're locked in on firing you no matter what, see #1 above. From what you've written I'd say you should just start looking for jobs right now, go ahead and interview for them, and hope to find something so you can quit instead of being "fired".

Sympathies, Anon. It does suck, but that's what tech companies like to do. Is the company firing a lot of people? They may be in financial trouble. Or maybe it's personal because some manager decided to be an asshole to you. Generally there's no way to fight this sort of stupid shit, and trying will only cause you a bunch of frustration and maybe financial loss.

62326503
>>62325409
Dame thing happened to my wife in 2023. Don't over exert yourself. They already are planning to fire you and are simply squeezing the last amount of juice they can from you.

It means the company is having issues with payroll and unfortunately you aren't jewish, indian, or irreplacable.

62326507
>>62325409
>Fortune 500 Tech Job
>PIP
Let me guess.

62326587
>>62326507
Nah, it's Amazon. Those fucking kikes love to PIP.

62326859
>>62326313
No I'm white and born in Bulgaria
inb4 slav nigger

62326870
>>62326507
I'll give you a hint... they hate letting you repair what's yours

62326897
>>62326047
>>62325749
I was gone for a bit banging gears on my bike and clearing my head. Ran into a big titty latina at the gas station.

Yeah I'm going to look into this. At the very least I'll draw out my time as much as I can and milk it. Financially I should be fine. I've actually got someone renting my home at the moment and I'm back at my parents place. Also got 5 figures worth of boomer rocks and some other investments.

I kept telling myself I'd pay off the mortgage on my primary (farm on 20 acres) and then leave the industry as a whole and pursue something on my own. Maybe I'll just start that now instead. Worst case scenario I'll have to liquidate some assets but I won't be homeless

62326905
>>62326870
You deserve pain if you work for Apple.

62326936
>>62325409
>I've got some investments I can liquidate to help me out with bills until I figure something out or get another job.
Looking for another job while being employed increases your value anon.

Don't ragequit, just quietly look for something else. Write down their concerns and when you leave don't burn bridges but be extremely polite and act as if there is not much more there is to do for you.

62326945
>>62326905
not apple

62327274
>>62325409
Nothing to do with performance, it's attitude.

62327286
>>62325483
>got placed on it
Supposed to refuse dummy.

62327326
>>62325409
Pip means you'll be fired soon.

62327609
>>62325409
OIL THE CANNONS, SHITANON!!

62327634
>>62325573
This.
Start looking for new employment ASAP. If they gave you a PIP and if they aren't willing to actually steer you in the correct direction, that means they already canned you but they have to go through the motions if they want the new H1B to replace you. It sucks, I know.

62328332
they're prepping to lay you off. its really that simple. you seem stuck on the why, and honestly it doesnt matter much. maybe you make more than others in your position. maybe someone higher up doesnt like you. ultimately doesn't really matter, do the stuff they tell you to do and get your resume ready and start applying for new jobs, thats whats within your control.

62328418
>>62328332
Fuck that faggot shit...just be a leech and poop on the floor on your way out. Rub poison oak on all the toilet seats. Stink bomb in the hvac. Release termites...etc

62329338
Let me guess, indian manager? Guess they want to hire their jeet friends

62329399
>>62325535
meet with your manager's manager and see what they say

item.title: If someone doesn't pump LINK by 15% in the next 27 hours it will have a weekly close below the trendline it has held sin… — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If someone doesn't pump LINK by 15% in the next 27 hours it will have a weekly close below the trendline it has held sin…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-06 21:09 UTC

OP

If someone doesn't pump LINK by 15% in the next 27 hours it will have a weekly close below the trendline it has held since 2018. And it will be over.

Someone with deep pockets needs to help us out

Early replies (first 3)

62329214
>>62329209
Your shitcoin doesn’t know you love it, no need to hold it further

62329221
>>62329209
meme lines dont mean anything

62329222
op supercuck hodl it all the way to zero with your limp pecker in your wrist

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329238
>>62329221
Every trader with a triple digit IQ would look for a short entry if it closed below that trendline. You dont think that will affect the price?

62329246
>>62329209
It’s almost over for good

62329267
>>62329238
every crypto has been shorted to hell for years now, doesnt matter

62329305
>>62329267
>the most important indicator every market participant with a brain uses to deduce long term trend doesnt matter to the price

62329319
>>62329305
yeah sure bro, go short & long based on ur meme lines

62329343
>>62329319
Go look what happened to Cardano after it broke below its 2018 trendline. Straight drop because anyone with a brain started shorting it into the ground, and now it's down almost 60% from that point in 6 months

62329405
>>62329343
you haven't even drawn us a graph with meme lines so stfu

62329410
>>62329343
Cardano is just dead shitcoin with no one using it

Thats why its finally dying.

62329438
>>62329405
You need someone else to draw 1 straight line underneath the weekly candles for you?

62329508
>>62329438

item.title: Crypto Holocaust — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto Holocaust

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-06 21:05 UTC

OP

Crypto is in an overinflated speculative bubble which has been pumped up by scammers and retards. The industry has lost sight of the fundamentals which set it up for success. Now we must pay.

BTC will break down
ETH will break down
All alts will break down

The forest floor is littered with waste. All filth must be cleansed by fire. Only the strongest will come back, but it will take years

Early replies (first 3)

62328265
The developers of these networks didn't have a functional product when they launched. Investors, who barely understand how any of this technology works, bought. The funny thing is, most of these investors will sell just before the technology is fully functional for global adoption. I would wager that quite a few people, who sold recently, are going to kill themselves when the market skyrockets in the coming weeks.

62328283
>>62328265
We will go lower. Institutions will buy retail capitulation (quality projects). Bags will be pumped after accumulation

62328293
>>62328283
Perhaps, but perhaps institutions inject far more liquidity into the market than retail ever has.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62328297
>>62328265
>the market skyrockets in the coming weeks
Weeks? What would be the catalyst?

62328325
>>62328297
Also I don’t understand how

> Only the strongest will come back, but it will take years
while
>the market skyrockets in the coming weeks

Weeks or years?

62328347
>>62328325
Years. Anon has not come to terms with reality yet

62328416
>>62328347
Sorry, you guys have the same ID color so I thought it was the same Anon

62329375
>>62328297
>CLARITY
>US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve
I'm not going to spoon feed you as to why these are the 2 biggest catalysts the crypto market has ever had.

62329437
>>62329375
The Trump administration and the market are both fickle. Suspiciousness remains a must.

62329484
>>62328178
I think crypto has no reason to even exist and it never did desu. The mythology of crypto is what drives price action, not utility.

62329493
>>62329484
Try thinking harder?

item.title: Are food trucks/stands profitable ? I’m a good cook and love to BBQ i wonder what startup costs and profits would look l… — 9 replies — 25.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Are food trucks/stands profitable ? I’m a good cook and love to BBQ i wonder what startup costs and profits would look l…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-06 20:51 UTC

OP

Are food trucks/stands profitable ? I’m a good cook and love to BBQ i wonder what startup costs and profits would look like. Anyone have experience running your own food stand?

Early replies (first 3)

62328822
>>62328814
My fear would be teens trying to knock it over or run off without paying

62328840
>>62328822
Yeah. I could see that being location specific problem. I was thinking dispensaries have taco trucks in my town and other states I’ve been to and stoners are slow. Community college was another location option and i could see that being more likely to experience grab and dashes. I’ll just need everyone pay before i hand them the food and maybe get a truck they can’t easily flip

62328915
>>62328814
Dont do it. Stupid high startup costs for something like BBQ. Youd be working 60hrs a week to make 80k/yr after spending 50k on startup.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329025
>>62328915
>over 1/3 profit in first year
That’s really good. What business do you suggest that can beat that?

62329043
>>62328814
This pic is from pakistan, not India

62329135
>>62329043
How do you know?

62329307
>>62329025
>What business do you suggest that can beat that?
they cant
glowies just shit on everything

62329424
>>62329043
Good morning sir

62329430
>>62328814
>getting permits and being in code depending on the county you're in can be a bitch
>big events like cons often want a cut of your sales on top of you usually having to compete with other trucks
>getting your name out can be difficult so you'll have to contact all kinds of customers and get leads for smaller events that risk not being profitable
>if you have staff then be ready to deal with some absolute retards because "lol service industry"
>food trucks are a pain in the ass to drive and maintain
A BBQ truck would be popular at any event but a safer bet might be towing a smoker on a trailer if you happen to have a vehicle that can haul it around.

item.title: 6 DOLLARS — 4 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
6 DOLLARS

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-06 20:49 UTC

OP

KEK BAGGIES

Early replies (first 3)

62327248
>>62327243
It just gets worse and worse for them.

crypto a shit.

62327252
I love the slow realization of all these bagholders that their staking and holding is just going to pay the salaries of chainlink labs as they sell off the token lol, they're so shameless about it.

there is no incentive for it to go up, retards. the fair value is probably sub $5 to operate oracles, it has no reason to go up.

62327413
Let's HECKIN GO
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329419
>>62327243
it's like a gift from Sergey at these prices

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 143 replies — 572.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 143 • Updated: 2026-06-06 20:57 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62319921
>>62319875
Meanwhile over half the coins with higher market caps are literally useless yet more valuable.

62322856
>>62318952
>>>The BTC out in market came from miners selling
>How is this different from Sergey selling?
>Or Sergey distributing to nodes who then sell?

https://etherscan.io/address/0x9b1c1752b6b6eaf62a8c491a8681b32c1f7ecde9#tokentxns

Can you prove that all the link tokens Chainlink Labs moved from their noncirculating token wallets to Binance went straight to the node operators? If you can't prove it, your analogy falls apart.

62323005
>>62316188
>Why did XLM pump +80% on lesser DTCC news then?

i don't know dude, maybe bc their liquidity is pretty thin

how many times do i have to fucking tell you people that the market isn't fair

you are essentially complaining that your scam isn't working as good as it used to. you realize that right?

>>62316251
>Yes anon, Bitcoin dumping every time Link has big news is my fault.

you are proving my point
if you're convinced there's a conspiracy to stop your gains, why would you continue holding
you are basically saying you know for sure it's impossible to make money, and yet you continue the same path

einstein would refer to that as insanity

62323033
>>62316263
>XLM is still higher than before the pump started, LINK prints new monthly lows
>if what the shill claimed was true, LINK would exhibit similar performance


the kid you are arguing against actually thinks he can debate his way into changing reality

i don't know if it's a combination of autism, naivety, or he's just plain retarded, but that's his thought process. he literally believes if he can show people how the market is "wronging" him, it will change things

62323065
>>62316401
>I bought in prior to mainnet so I am well into the green

fact check: you're down 80% and desperate

>and am living off my staking rewards.

LOL

fact check: you have enough money to survive 5-10 years and then will move back home if you have even left yet

>I sympathize with you anon since if this were XRP,

fact check: it's not xrp

> You're doomed to wait till after the Clarity Act

fact check: clarity act will have zero upward pressure on link price

>when banks finally get their markets up and running

what the fuck does that even mean. banks run fine every single day

>Real World Assets NFT treading

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHA

62323184
>>62316444
>Chainlink network must use the LINK token to secure the network

fact check: chainlink network currently operates fine without link token. (offshore labs company makes offchain deals and full staking version with slashing not released nor coming any time soon)

>>62316605
>. This program is a way to grow these companies, the actual users of the network and get them up and running and profitable which would drive more usage of the Chainlink network which should drive the value up.

fact check: these companies are not growing. they are grifts with no users, no profit, and have no value accrual to the link token

>>62316776
>duuuude chicken and eggs

that's hilarious zach still posts here. you belong in jail

62323264
>>62317156
>oh cool adem's here. serious question why do you live on this board 24/7?

don't project you little fat skinny weirdo

62323308
>>62317182
>you're very smart, because while i read you i have thoughts that come up, and you answer those thoughts. bene lurking 4chan since 2007 or some shit i've learnt over the years to spot smart / legit people, and you are one, it's rare to meet good people


fact check: wow this is getting pretty gay

>>62317182
>they keep fudding for absolutely no reason for all these years

what exactly do you mean by the term "fud"
are you talking about saying what the literal price is, or what's actually going on? they call that reality

>>62317656
>duuuude the world went to shit after covid
>To bring in this new system that Link facilitates. And we get to be on top while the trash get fucked. It’ll be a lot more meaningful cuz we will be able to dab.

HAHAHAHAAHAHAA

62323333
So many deranged cuckolds are going to HODL this shitcoin until its back to $2

62323719
when does the humiliation end, stinkies
when do you finally rise up and say, NO

62324418
>>62323333
checked
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62324495
I'm new to this whole "investing" thing but isn't holding an asset that's down 70% over 5 years, like, a really bad investment? Or is it good that it goes down because you can make more money losing money like those Hollywood studios do? Just seems weird to still like and hold a thing so much that seemingly only brings you pain and suffering.

62324507
>>62314036
Why would CLL want the token to be dirt cheap when they are the primary holders and benefactors. Why a slow bleed when they could have a massive run up and dump eventually?

62324652
>>62324507
they don't need the token to appreciate in value, why? because the private deals they make bring em steady revenu that is independent of the link price, because they subsidize the network using link tokens, that are preminted, lets say to run the network for a wholz ass year it costs 50k link in fees, they get paid their 300M$ of scale money no matter how much the network fees are, they dont care they dont buy the link, in essence they are basically indirectly selling their link for cash through private deals and way higher than market price

62324749
>this board is still composed of linkie cuckolds in the big 2026.
Why?

62324777
>>62324495
generally speaking, yes. But you don't go by rule of thumbs if you're looking for those once in a lifetime golden egg investments. Chainlink is a special case in that they're the only crypto company banks are integrating their technologies with.

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62324797
>>62324749
Why? Because 4chan is a safe place for all kinds of losers. (I love you all)

62324959
>>62323184
lol if I was CLG I wouldn't have to post here I'd be retired because that faggot grifted his way to where he is. The irony though of you always assuming everyone is your twitter e-celebs (the verbose xrp shill does the same with fishy iirc), meanwhile you literally post here every day, same prose, same twitter screencaps, often quoting your own twitter account. Its just bizarre.

62325222
>>62324777
checked! thanks for posting the dtcc link.
https://x.com/TomZschach/status/2062907080931946674
https://x.com/SmartContract/status/2062670460412625036
big banks including jp morgan announced they want to launch a tokenized deposit network next year also.
Means DTCC which settles stocks AND the big banks need Chainlink

62325301
>>62325222
>>62324777
nice, very nice
But yes, while you can point to 5 years down 70% and be pissy, you can also say, for something with solid fundamentals, its the perfect time to buy. All a question of if crypto is legitimate or remains a scam casino.

62327001
>>62322856
I don't have to prove that at all, you specifically said the mechanism whereby token from the uncirculating supply are used to pay the nodes "decouples the link price from the usage of the network".

62327952
>>62327001
I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.

You are trying to make the BTC mining comparison but it falls apart. You can say CLL paying from team wallets is the same as miners mining BTC, but miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block. Node operators do not decide their rates of payment, CLL does. Miners will charge to maintain operations and so BTC's price is intrinsically tied to the cost of operations. After all, no new BTC enters the market outside of miners thus the BTC's price tag will be influenced by operation costs. >>62322856's etherscan wallet proves new Link that enters the market does not go through node operators first meaning that they're stripped of influencing the price from operational costs. This is what I mean by the Link token being decoupled from the network.

Are you deliberately being obtuse? You're not even addressing my points or logic.

62327991
>>62324959
>the team of paid KOLs whom use 4chan lingo and memes do not post on /biz/ guise!!!!
yeah, sure lil buddy

62328404
test

62328534
>>62327952
>I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.
That's what I said you said.

>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
That's completely false.

You're clueless. A clueless ape.

62328546
>>62328404
Testing 1…2…3

62328573
>>62324495
Everything and everyone is going to be flushed in the crypto world and traditional world. And then the new gay system sprouts up from Chainlink. There’s going to be a lot of people out there who made it in other cryptos who then decided to invest their shit like NPC’s into index funds or whatever getting muuh 4% return and are going to be wiped out. The 4% stakers will get isn’t anything remarkable on its face compared to anything else out there except it’s going to be the baseline of the new financial system.

62328670
>>62327991
Maybe he still does, I don't know, I'm saying he's a faggot grifter, other poster (who is obviously adem) was accusing me of being him. Unless you're also him just changing your id, which wouldn't surprise me on this dead board.

62329193
>>62312690
I Sold it all for BTC, I staked some recently, didnt believe in staking before because of wrapping and bridge but with TBV that's can be bypassed. I'm all out of LINK, I hold for years but nothing to show for

62329208
>>62329193
same

62326816
>>62326811

62326992

62326998
>>62326790

delusion

62327000
>>62326998
It'll never not be funny that thr black dude who played barney wrote sex domination books

62327003
Bobos tried so hard but after that rejection its over

62327004
>>62327003
Its just a little free bleed anon... I got a heavy cycle coming

62327088
>>62327004
incredible

62327122
>>62326811
this

62327127
you mean bitcoin will never reach $100k for the rest of humanity.

62327134
>>62327127
Depends if they manage killing eth.

62327142
>>62326790
Double-bottom at a higher level, went through the downwards trendline and bounced off of it upwards. All in and if this isn't it, then I will call every fucking whale in the world a perma-faggot and eat my gun.

62327327
nobody believes in crypto anymore it was a fad

62327335
>>62327327
enough bottom signaling

62327394
You Crypto bro's are mostly retarded,
This week we entered an early defensive cycle with multiple diving factors; implied volatility, SpaceX IPO, recession fears, war.
Pension funds and other defensives expect upcoming volatility and pulled into background staples; Consumer Defensive, Utilities, Treasuries etc. Sovereign Wealth is doing a spread; Tbonds, MMF's, Staples, Growth, just riding it out. Hedge funds are coiling liquidity from speculative assets mostly to MMF's coming from things like Crypto, Semi's etc. Quants are just cashing the waves and stealing off every peak.
The outlook isn't good, Governments are outspending and raising treasury volumes competing globally and driving yields, consumer debt is high so MMF's are cashing on repos and commercial paper, t-buybacks won't happen because it won't help anything until OCR drops and that's not on the cards.
TLDR; there's no liquidity to buy something like Crypto.

62327696
>>62327394
Gm how do I learn how to understand macro like this autist?

62327709
>>62327394
Oh well back to plebbit with you nigger

62327712
>>62327394
how soon do you think we'll get a fed rate hike?

62327725
>>62327712
I dunno, that seems to be a political question > financial. MMF's will be driving t-bills lower so that can stave it off in the short term but they will be forced to raise OCR, when? Dunno, before Christmas.

62327738
>>62327725
*Earlier if the war ramps up spending

62327740
>>62327725
Give me resources please bro I don’t want to be poor anymore

62327756
>>62327709
shut the fuck up

62327804
>>62327740
Just start from the bottom. Learn how money is created, then follow it all the way up. Bond markets, Government spending/debt, consumer behaviour and REIT, Market cycles, risk-on risk-off, MMF's, different types of institutions, market behaviour of said institutions, cycle structures, then expand into surface level stuff like volatility, speculation, options etc.

62327944
>Violently accepted
You mean struggles to hold it while already having dipped below again twice.

62327957
>>62326811

62327972

>there's no liquidity to buy something like Crypto

2. There’s no liquidity… yet
3. Don’t assume everyone else is a retard, you will lose a lot of money with that attitude

62328548
>>62327756

62328554
>>62326811

62329224
>>62328554

item.title: We're in a bubble. — 12 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
We're in a bubble.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-06 19:05 UTC

OP

>Watched CNBC live after years
>90% of ads are about AI
>Ad about buying life insurance
>Ad about selling your life insurance
>Some old ass dude selling his SpaceX IPO on his website pre-IPO date to exit scam before the stock crashes

Explain to me how we're not in a AI bubble? Yesterday everyone in the knows are exiting because they know SpaceX will crash the markets. Then other IPO's like OpenAI and Anthropic crash it even more. They want all retail exit liquidity before the stupidty dries up.

Sell before the IPO or you'll regret it.

Early replies (first 3)

62327775
>>62327768
Haha nice try I’m gonna buy even more ai

62327776
they know it, but it's about whether the retail baggies know it. it's a bit more like dot com rather than 08' (considering your picrel).

>retail exit
there it is.

62327778
>>62327768
I’m buying the spacex ipo lol BaSeD ELoN 2 Da MoON

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62327787
>>62327777

62327844
>>62327768
Your consensus is were in a bubble because CNBC has life insurance commercials

The brain rot on this board is unreal

62328007
>>62327844

62328010
Uhh you see the jobs report is like strong and stuff which means uh we should sell everything cause like everyone having a job is like bad and stuff

62328013

62328049

62328063

>>62327844

>>62328049

62329012

62329030
>>62327768

62328538
It's not a terrible entry, definitely better than buying at $3k-4k, but Bitcoin is going to $25k by October, so if you wait a few more months you can buy ETH at $700-800

>>62328538
Im having trouble thinking it goes much lower than the 2017 high, I think best case scenario is we wick to 1201 and everyone with 1200 limits eats dick

62328898
